mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound signals sustained monetary accommodation, lowering the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and supporting a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 prices over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.22 near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during near-zero FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate remains above 7.5% following a sharp spike, it signals labor market slack consistent with an early-recovery regime; equity markets begin pricing in the eventual recovery, producing positive forward returns over a quarterly horizon as the rate begins to decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 7.5% and declining from a recent peak
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns following UNRATE>7.5% trigger episodes are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two distinct historical episodes, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in nonfarm payrolls growth and industrial production growth indicates a synchronized real-economy recovery that is not fully priced into equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; neither series alone captures this synchronized-recovery signal.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change > 0 and INDPRO 3-month change > 0 simultaneously
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with synchronized labor and industrial rebound
failure_condition: If the joint-trigger signal's forward 20d return distribution is not significantly different from the distribution when only one series is rising (Kolmogorov-Smirnov p > 0.10), reject the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects resilient consumer demand, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces a modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns following positive RSAFS growth are not significantly higher than following negative RSAFS growth (p > 0.05) across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime, a decline in housing starts signals weakening rate-sensitive demand that foreshadows broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon; in an easing regime the same decline signal is muted or absent.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS is rising
failure_condition: If negative HOUST momentum during FEDFUNDS increases does not precede below-median 60-day SPX returns in at least 60% of historical tightening-cycle instances, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rebound in industrial production above a 2% three-month annualized growth threshold indicates supply-side capacity recovery that supports corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If forward 20d SPX returns conditional on the INDPRO trigger are not significantly different from unconditional returns (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero federal funds rate and elevated unemployment above 7% reflects an emergency policy response to a demand shock; markets price a strong forward recovery as both easing and slack persist jointly, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon that exceeds what either series predicts independently.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with emergency monetary accommodation
failure_condition: If the joint FEDFUNDS-UNRATE trigger produces forward 60d returns statistically indistinguishable from the marginal effect of FEDFUNDS alone (interaction term coefficient not significant at p < 0.10 in regression), reject interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in nonfarm payrolls reflects continuing labor market repair and consumer income growth, which supports short-horizon equity buying pressure and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and forward 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.
